outputs.document.md:
# ADSL Analysis Variable Derivation Documentation

## 1. Treatment Variable Derivation Chain

### 1.1 TRT01P/TRT01PN Derivation from DM.ARMCD

The treatment variables in ADSL are derived from the DM domain as follows:

```
ADSL.TRT01P = DM.ARMCD
ADSL.TRT01PN = Decode(DM.ARMCD, treatment_code_mapping)
```

Where:
- `TRT01P` represents the planned treatment for period 01 as a code
- `TRT01PN` represents the planned treatment for period 01 as a decoded character value
- `treatment_code_mapping` is a mapping of treatment codes to their corresponding descriptions

Example mapping:
- "PLC" → "Placebo"
- "DRG10" → "Drug 10mg"
- "DRG20" → "Drug 20mg"

### 1.2 Treatment Sequence Variables

For multiple treatment periods, the following variables are derived:

```
ADSL.TRT02P = DM.ARMCD2
ADSL.TRT02PN = Decode(DM.ARMCD2, treatment_code_mapping)
```

## 2. Population Flag Criteria

### 2.1 Safety Analysis Population (SAFFL)

```
SAFFL = "Y" if subject meets all of the following criteria:
  - Subject received at least one dose of study treatment
  - Subject has at least one post-baseline safety assessment
SAFFL = "N" otherwise
```

### 2.2 Intent-to-Treat Population (ITTFL)

```
ITTFL = "Y" if subject meets all of the following criteria:
  - Subject was randomized
  - Subject has at least one post-baseline efficacy assessment
ITTFL = "N" otherwise
```

### 2.3 Per-Protocol Population (PPFL)

```
PPFL = "Y" if subject meets all of the following criteria:
  - ITTFL = "Y"
  - Subject completed the study without major protocol violations
  - Subject has a treatment compliance rate ≥ 80%
PPFL = "N" otherwise
```

### 2.4 Major Protocol Violation Flag

```
MAJPROVFL = "Y" if subject has any major protocol violations as determined by the medical monitor
MAJPROVFL = "N" otherwise
```

## 3. Baseline Measure Sources

### 3.1 Continuous Variables

For continuous variables, baseline is typically defined as the last non-missing assessment prior to first dose of study treatment:

```
BASE = Last non-missing value prior to or on the first dose date (EX.STDTM)
```

### 3.2 Categorical Variables

For categorical variables, baseline is typically defined as the last non-missing assessment prior to first dose of study treatment:

```
BASEC = Last non-missing categorical value prior to or on the first dose date (EX.STDTM)
```

### 3.3 Specific Variable Baselines

#### 3.3.1 Vital Signs

```
BASEx = Last non-missing VS record prior to or on the first dose date (EX.STDTM)
```

Where x represents the vital sign parameter (e.g., BASESYS for systolic blood pressure).

#### 3.3.2 Laboratory Values

```
BASEx = Last non-missing LB record prior to or on the first dose date (EX.STDTM)
```

Where x represents the laboratory test (e.g., BASEGLUC for glucose).

#### 3.3.3 ECG Parameters

```
BASEx = Last non-missing EG record prior to or on the first dose date (EX.STDTM)
```

Where x represents the ECG parameter (e.g., BASEQT for QT interval).

## 4. Disposition Flag Logic

### 4.1 Completion Status

```
COMPLFL = "Y" if subject completed the study as defined in the protocol
COMPLFL = "N" if subject discontinued early for any reason
```

### 4.2 Disposition Reason Flags

```
DSRAEFL = "Y" if subject discontinued due to adverse event
DSRDECFL = "Y" if subject discontinued due to decision by subject
DSRPROGFL = "Y" if subject discontinued due to disease progression
DSRLOSTFL = "Y" if subject was lost to follow-up
DSROTHFL = "Y" if subject discontinued for other reasons
```

### 4.3 Disposition Date

``
DCDT = Date of disposition (from DS domain)
```

### 4.4 Early Discontinuation Flag

```
EOSSTT = "COMPLETED" if COMPLFL = "Y"
EOSSTT = "DISCONTINUED" if COMPLFL = "N"
```

## 5. Additional Analysis Variables

### 5.1 Treatment Duration

```
TRTDUR = Last treatment date (EX.ENDTM) - First treatment date (EX.STDTM) + 1
```

### 5.2 Study Day

```
TRTSDT = Date of first treatment (EX.STDTM)
TRTEDT = Date of last treatment (EX.ENDTM)
```

### 5.3 Age at Randomization

```
AGE = Randomization date (RF.RFSTDTC) - Date of birth (DM.BRTHDTC)
```

### 5.4 Age Group

```
AGEGR1 = Age group based on AGE:
  "1" if AGE < 18
  "2" if 18 ≤ AGE < 65
  "3" if AGE ≥ 65
```

### 5.5 Sex

```
SEX = DM.SEX
```

### 5.6 Race

```
RACE = DM.RACE
```

### 5.7 Ethnicity

```
ETHNIC = DM.ETHNIC
```
